subject: Cloud Computing
title: AWS: The Service Explosion
description: A short lesson to help you apply AWS: The Service Explosion.

hook.text: AWS started with storage and compute. Now it offers robotics, satellite ground stations, and quantum computing.

buildup.text: Every year at re:Invent, AWS announces dozens of new services. The catalog grows relentlessly.

discovery.text: AWS's strategy: build a service for every imaginable workload so customers never need to leave.

twist.text: The sheer number of services overwhelms users. Even AWS experts only know a fraction of the catalog.

climax.text: Breadth is AWS's moat. Competitors can match a few services but nobody matches all 200+.

punchline.text: AWS has a service for that. Whatever 'that' is.

quiz.question: Why does AWS keep launching so many new services?
options: To cover every possible workload so customers never need another provider
Each service is required by a different government regulation
AWS replaces old services with new ones every year
